Now I have a clear picture:
- Left green (node 9): rows 33-35, cols 9-11, center white (node 11)
- Left frame (node 8): charcoal, boundary shows it's at rows 32-36, cols 11-15 (L-shaped, missing bottom-right corner)
- Left frame interior (node 10): white, rows 33-35, cols 12-14
- Right green (node 6): rows 30-32, cols 30-32, center charcoal (node 7)
- Right frame (node 4): charcoal, rows 26-30, cols 44-48
- Right frame interior (node 5): white, rows 27-29, cols 45-47
- Purple bar (node 2): rows 21-41, cols 33-38
So the left green is at cols 9-11, and the left frame interior is at cols 12-14. I need to move the left green RIGHT by about 3 columns (1 step) to get it into the frame.
Wait, looking at the left frame boundary more carefully: `[[32, 11], [32, 15], [36, 15], [36, 11], [36, 14], [35, 15], [33, 15], [32, 14]]` - this is an L-shaped frame with an opening on the right side. The interior is at cols 12-14, rows 33-35.
The left green is at cols 9-11, so I need to move it RIGHT by 3 columns (1 step) to get it to cols 12-14, which is the frame interior.
Let me click on the left green and move RIGHT.
For the right side, the right green is at cols 30-32, and the right frame interior is at cols 45-47. The purple bar is at cols 33-38, blocking the path. I need to figure out how to get past the purple bar.
Let me first fix the left green, then work on the right side.
